evidence: IDR/idr_analysis.py main() function is 80+ lines and handles: config resolution, file-path defaulting, data loading, TA filtering, whole-dataset processing, DisProt matching, checkpoint detection, disorder prediction — multiple distinct responsibilities in one body

app/app.py filtered_data() reactive applies 6 independent filter conditions sequentially in 60+ lines; each condition block repeats the same operator-dispatch pattern — the function does one thing (filter) but its implementation does not leverage the repeated structure
analysis/non_srp_filter.py and analysis/srp_filter.py __main__ blocks: 40+ lines each handling load, merge, filter, group-by, save — all inline, no sub-functions
protein_analysis_utils.py summary_report() has a reasonable single responsibility: print a summary report; the inner loop (for col in compare_columns) is appropriate nesting
IDR/nterm_analysis.py functions are consistently single-responsibility and compact; compute_kappa() and compute_omega() are long but genuinely complex mathematical functions with a single purpose
IDR/idr_analysis.py _predict_one() is a clean single-responsibility function: fetch sequence + run worker prediction + print status
app/app.py server() function defines all Shiny callbacks inline as nested functions — appropriate for Shiny's reactive paradigm; the nesting is framework-required

strengths: IDR/nterm_analysis.py is the best-designed module: functions are single-purpose, parameter lists are minimal, and the analysis pipeline composes cleanly
protein_analysis_utils.py core functions (filter_and_compare, filter_by_cterm_distance, compare_categories) are coherently scoped
_predict_one() in IDR/idr_analysis.py is a well-extracted single-responsibility function
design_coherence.issue_character: The design coherence issue is concentrated in orchestration entry points: IDR/idr_analysis.py main() conflates configuration resolution, data loading, and branched processing into a single 80+ line function; the non_srp/srp __main__ blocks are similarly undecomposed.
design_coherence.score_rationale: At the function/class level, the codebase is mostly coherent. The IDR nterm_analysis.py module is well-designed. The problem is at the orchestration layer: main() in IDR/idr_analysis.py and the __main__ blocks in the filter scripts take on too many responsibilities without decomposition. The design coherence of analysis scripts also suffers from the print-mixed-with-computation pattern. Score is in the middle range.

issues.summary: IDR/idr_analysis.py main() handles config resolution, data loading, TA filtering, whole-dataset labelling, DisProt matching, and disorder predictions in a single 80+ line function
evidence: main() resolves config and output_path, calls pd.read_csv twice (data + disprot), conditionally calls filter_ta_proteins or adds is_ta column, calls match_disprot, conditionally calls run_disorder_predictions — 5 distinct concerns
The if/else branch for filter_ta vs whole-dataset adds ~20 lines of divergent logic inside the function
issues.suggestion: Extract a _load_inputs(config) function returning (df, disprot_df), a _apply_ta_filter(df, config) function, and keep main() as a thin orchestrator calling them in sequence

issues.summary: non_srp_filter.py and srp_filter.py __main__ blocks inline all pipeline steps without decomposition
evidence: 40+ line __main__ block in each file: load CSV, rename columns, merge, filter Prediction, add TMD/IMD counts, groupby, save multiple CSVs — all inline
analysis/improved_analysis.py demonstrates the better pattern with named helper functions (_run_basic_comparisons, _save_filtered_datasets)
issues.suggestion: Extract named functions for each pipeline step (load_and_merge, classify_by_membrane_count, save_by_group) following the pattern established in improved_analysis.py

root_causes: main() in IDR/idr_analysis.py grew by accretion as new pipeline stages were added without refactoring the entry point
filter script __main__ blocks were written as single-use pipeline scripts where decomposition was not prioritized
